description: angr is a platform-agnostic binary analysis framework built on top of symbolic execution engines. Useful for automated reverse engineering and CTF challenges.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/angr && python3 angr.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/angr && python3 angr.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/angr && python3 angr.py --help`. Platform-agnostic binary analysis framework.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the exploit category. Fits when the engagement needs: Platform-agnostic binary analysis framework.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Platform-agnostic binary analysis framework.

name: angr

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
